rationale: Complete, correct proof via the open-cover characterisation with clear logical steps and proper use of closedness, but lacks discussion of assumptions (e.g. Hausdorff not needed), alternative proof strategies, or confidence calibration on generalisability.
rubric_evidence: Since F is closed, X\setminus F is open, and {V_i}∪{X\setminus F} is an open cover of X
By compactness of X, there exist finitely many indices
Thus every open cover of F has a finite subcover, so F is compact
